title: Podman - Detect when net.ipv4.ip_forward=0 and warn user
body:
If net.ipv4.ip_forward is not enabled then the Podman/libpod containers will not be able to reach the internet with the packets being dropped.

In my case (clean Ubuntu environment), it was due to net.ipv4.ip_forward not being enabled.

My solution was to enable forwarding which allowed the containers to reach the internet.

```
echo 'net.ipv4.ip_forward=1' >> /etc/sysctl.conf
sudo sysctl -p
```

I think Podman should warn users when ip_forward hasn't been set.

comments.body: This is easy enough to detect (it's exposed as `/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward`). I wonder if we shouldn't upstream this to the OCICNI package or CNI plugins themselves, as this is something that would also affect Kubernetes.
Lets get a quick fix into podman, and then work on getting it into OCICNI.  That way we can help our users who hit this issue.
@BenHall  you must use a cni configuration file that has isGateway: true in it.  that will automatically flip the forwarding bit for you.  We ship a default cni configuration file in podman.
yes, in the podman default configuration -> https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/master/cni/87-podman-bridge.conflist#L8
@baude Just checked the environment. The file was in the correct location.
@BenHall i tested on xenail ... what are you on?
@baude Ubuntu 16.04.2 LTS. I'll double check the environment without the IP forward flag
Are we just saying that this works on Xenail?
the long and short on this is that ip_forward is enabled on the fly by CNI.  There's little podman can do here because podman execs cni.
